California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Elvin W. Moon, President 
E. W. Moon, Incorporated 
January 24, 1989 
8939 South Sepulveda Blvd., Suite 304 
Los Angeles, 
CA 
90045 
Dear Mr. Moon: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-461 
This is to confirm your telephone conversation of January 4, 
1989, with Jeanne Pritchard concerning your campaign reporting 
obligations under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act,,}.1/ 
QUESTION 
As a candidate for Vice-Chair of the California Democratic 
Party, what campaig~ reports, if any, are you required to file? 
CONCLUSION: 
A candidate for vice-chair of the California Democratic Party 
has no campaign filing obligations under the Political Reform Act. 
In addition, no other campaign provisions of the Act apply to the 
candidate. 
ANALYSIS 
Under the provisions of the Act, persons who hold state or 
local elective office and candidates for such office are required 
to file campaign disclosure statements. 
section 82023 states 
that: 
"Elective office" means any state, regional, county, 
municipal district or judicial office .... 
"Elective office" 
also includes membership on a county central committee .... " 
Further, section 82022 defines election to mean 
II ••• any 
primary, general, special or recall election .... " 
Pursuant to 
two sections, the elected offices of the Cali 
ia 
Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2. 
ion 6 of the California Code of~Regulations. 
J


Elvin W. Moon 
Page 2 
Democratic Party and the election process by which they are filled 
do not fall under the provisions of the political Reform Act. 
Therefore, candidates for such offices are not subject to the 
Act's campaign provisions. 
If you have any questions please call me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane Griffiths 
General Counsel 
ilL 
By: 
Kim M. Taylor 
Staff Services Analyst
